Just now

--

Kubernetes has become the de facto standard for container orchestration, and with its widespread adoption, interview expectations have evolved significantly. Companies are no longer interested in candidates who only understand basic concepts like Pods, Deployments, and Services. They want engineers who can troubleshoot production incidents, design highly available systems, and understand Kubernetes internals.
Production-focused Kubernetes interviews are typically scenario-based. Interviewers often present real incidents and expect candidates to explain not only what happened but also how they would diagnose and resolve the problem.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Amy Hirschi on UnsplashBelow are fifteen important Kubernetes production interview questions and detailed answers that frequently appear in DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering, and Platform Engineering interviews.
1. What Happens If an InitContainer Fails with restartPolicy: Never?
Init containers are special containers that execute before the main application containers start. They are commonly used for:

• Database initialization

• Waiting for dependencies

• Downloading configuration files
